2604.09474.title: SafeMind: A Risk-Aware Differentiable Control Framework for Adaptive and Safe Quadruped Locomotion
2604.09474.paper_summary: Learning-based quadruped controllers achieve impressive agility but typically lack formal safety guarantees under model uncertainty, perception noise, and unstructured contact conditions. We introduce SafeMind, a differentiable stochastic safety-control framework that unifies probabilistic Control Barrier Functions with semantic context understanding and meta-adaptive risk calibration. SafeMind explicitly models epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty through a variance-aware barrier constraint embedded in a differentiable quadratic program, thereby preserving gradient flow for end-to-end training. A semantics-to-constraint encoder modulates safety margins using perceptual or language cues, while a meta-adaptive learner continuously adjusts risk sensitivity across environments. We provide theoretical conditions for probabilistic forward invariance, feasibility, and stability under stochastic dynamics. SafeMind is deployed on Unitree A1 and ANYmal C at 200~Hz and validated across 12 terrain types, dynamic obstacles, morphology perturbations, and semantically defined tasks. Experiments show that SafeMind reduces safety violations by 3--10x and energy consumption by 10--15% relative to state-of-the-art CBF, MPC, and hybrid RL baselines, while maintaining real-time control performance.
2604.09474.paper_summary_zh: 學習基礎的四足控制器實現了令人印象深刻的靈活性，但通常在模型不確定性、感知噪音和非結構接觸條件下缺乏正式的安全保證。我們介紹了 SafeMind，一個可微分的隨機安全控制框架，將概率控制障礙函數與語義上下文理解和元自適應風險校準統一。SafeMind 通過嵌入在可微分二次規劃中的方差感知障礙約束，明確建模了認知不確定性和隨機不確定性，從而保留了端到端訓練的梯度流。一個語義到約束的編碼器使用感知或語言線索調節安全邊際，而元自適應學習者則在不同環境中持續調整風險敏感度。我們提供了在隨機動力學下概率前向不變性、可行性和穩定性的理論條件。SafeMind 在 Unitree A1 和 ANYmal C 上以 200 Hz 部署，並在 12 種地形類型、動態障礙物、形態擾動和語義定義的任務中進行驗證。實驗表明，與最先進的 CBF、MPC 和混合 RL 基準相比，SafeMind 將安全違規減少了 3 到 10 倍，能耗降低了 10 到 15%，同時保持實時控制性能。
2604.09474.author: Zukun Zhang et.al.
2604.09474.authors: Zukun Zhang, Kai Shu, Mingqiao Mo
